- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

1	"""
2	Phoenix observability bootstrap for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #2).
3	
4	ADR-0009: Phoenix is the local, self-hosted LLM-tracing stack (LangSmith was
5	disqualified as SaaS-only). The Phoenix server runs on the compute node and
6	agent LLM calls export OpenTelemetry traces to it.
7	
8	Two responsibilities, deliberately separated:
9	
10	  1. The SERVER is a long-lived process — run it via the `phoenix serve` CLI
11	     (see launch_server_command()) or the launchd plist in scripts/. It is NOT
12	     launched from inside the application process.
13	
14	  2. INSTRUMENTATION is wired per-process via bootstrap_observability(), called
15	     once by each run entrypoint. It registers a global OTel tracer provider
16	     (BatchSpanProcessor — spans are buffered and exported in the background, so
17	     a momentary server outage drops spans rather than blocking or failing the
18	     run) pointed at the running server, and turns on the LangChain instrumentor.
19	     Because the provider is registered globally, the spans the reasoning_client
20	     emits for BOTH tiers — including Tier-3 (claude-agent-sdk), which LangChain
21	     instrumentation does not see — export to Phoenix too.
22	
23	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): wrap a run in run_trace_context(run_id) so
24	every span produced during the run carries the run_id as session.id + metadata,
25	cross-referencing MLflow / PostgresSaver / PROV-DM.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import signal
34	import sys
35	import threading
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	
38	# ADR-0002: traces carry SCF-derived content and must never leave the machine.
39	# The loopback guard is the single canonical validator in config.py, shared with
40	# Qdrant and MLflow (pass-5) so the data-sovereignty boundary is enforced
41	# identically everywhere; 0.0.0.0 is rejected (it binds all interfaces).
42	from ..config import LOOPBACK_HOSTS as _LOOPBACK_HOSTS  # noqa: F401  (kept for callers/tests)
43	from ..config import load_platform_config
44	from ..config import require_loopback as _require_loopback
45	
46	_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# Cached registered provider — registration is idempotent within a process
49	# (repeat bootstrap calls return the same provider instead of double-registering
50	# the instrumentor, which would duplicate every span).
51	_PROVIDER = None
52	
53	
54	def _phoenix_cfg() -> dict:
55	    return load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"]
56	
57	
58	def phoenix_base_url() -> str:
59	    """Return the Phoenix server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6006."""
60	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
61	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
62	
63	
64	def phoenix_endpoint() -> str:
65	    """Return the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint the tracer provider exports to.
66	
67	    Validates loopback at the source (pass-5 #obs-wiring): this is public API and
68	    is called from the __main__ entrypoint and tests, not only from the already-
69	    guarded instrument_langchain(), so it must enforce its own ADR-0002 precondition."""
70	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
71	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "Phoenix OTLP export")
72	    return f"{phoenix_base_url()}/v1/traces"
73	
74	
75	def launch_server_command() -> str:
76	    """Return the shell command that starts the persistent Phoenix server.
77	
78	    Validates the bind host is loopback (#283): the server bind, not just the
79	    client export host, is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box.
80	    """
81	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
82	    host = _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "server bind")
83	    return f"phoenix serve --host {host} --port {cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
84	
85	
86	def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
87	    """
88	    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
89	    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.
90	
91	    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
92	    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).
93	
94	    Returns the tracer provider.
95	    """
96	    global _PROVIDER
97	    if _PROVIDER is not None:
98	        return _PROVIDER
99	
100	    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
101	    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
102	    from phoenix.otel import register
103	
104	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
105	    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
106	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")
107	
108	    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
109	    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
110	    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
111	    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
112	    # operator override.
113	    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
114	
115	    provider = register(
116	        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
117	        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
118	        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
119	        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
120	    )
121	    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
122	    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
123	    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
124	    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
125	    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
126	    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
127	    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
128	        _log.warning(
129	            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
130	            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
131	            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
132	            "OTel instrumentation."
133	        )
134	    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
135	    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
136	    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
137	    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
138	    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
139	    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
140	    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
141	        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
142	        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
143	    _PROVIDER = provider
144	    return provider
145	
146	
147	def _install_sigterm_flush(provider) -> None:
148	    """Flush + shut down the span processor on SIGTERM before the process exits.
149	
150	    Signal handlers can only be installed from the main thread; if we're not on it
151	    (e.g. instrumentation wired from a worker), skip silently — atexit still covers
152	    the clean-exit and SIGINT paths."""
153	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():
154	        return
155	
156	    def _handler(_signum, _frame):
157	        try:
158	            provider.force_flush()
159	            if hasattr(provider, "shutdown"):
160	                provider.shutdown()
161	        finally:
162	            sys.exit(0)
163	
164	    try:
165	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	    except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - non-main-thread / unsupported
167	        pass
168	
169	
170	def bootstrap_observability():
171	    """
172	    Idempotently wire tracing for the current process. Called once by each run
173	    entrypoint (graph runners). Degrades to a no-op — never raising — when tracing
174	    is disabled by config, switched off via HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING, or the
175	    instrumentation stack cannot be initialized. Returns the provider or None.
176	    """
177	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip():
178	        return None
179	    try:
180	        cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
181	    except Exception:
182	        return None
183	    if not cfg.get("enabled", True):
184	        return None
185	    # Also point MLflow at the local store so experiment tracking is actually
186	    # configured in a run path (cross-store run_id correlation, #282). Best-effort.
187	    try:
188	        from ..tracking.mlflow_setup import configure_mlflow
189	        configure_mlflow()
190	    except Exception:
191	        pass
192	    try:
193	        return instrument_langchain()
194	    except Exception:
195	        # Missing deps, server unreachable at registration, etc. — tracing is
196	        # best-effort observability and must never block the research run.
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	@contextmanager
201	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None):
202	    """
203	    Context manager that stamps run_id onto every span produced within it, for
204	    cross-store correlation. Sets it two ways so BOTH span sources pick it up:
205	    OpenInference using_attributes (LangChain auto-spans → session.id) AND OTel
206	    baggage `hcgrc.run_id`, which the reasoning_client seam reads as a fallback so
207	    its manual T2/T3 spans carry the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it
208	    explicitly (#239). No-op if run_id is None or the helpers are unavailable.
209	    """
210	    if not run_id:
211	        yield
212	        return
213	    from contextlib import ExitStack
214	
215	    from opentelemetry import baggage, context
216	
217	    with ExitStack() as stack:
218	        try:
219	            from openinference.instrumentation import using_attributes
220	            stack.enter_context(using_attributes(session_id=run_id, metadata={"run_id": run_id}))
221	        except Exception:
222	            pass
223	        token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("hcgrc.run_id", run_id))
224	        try:
225	            yield
226	        finally:
227	            context.detach(token)
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
231	    print(f"Phoenix base URL:    {phoenix_base_url()}")
232	    print(f"OTLP traces endpoint: {phoenix_endpoint()}")
233	    print(f"Start the server with: {launch_server_command()}")
234

1	"""
2	MLflow tracking setup for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #1).
3	
4	DATA SOVEREIGNTY (ADR-0002): MLflow runs against a LOCAL SQLITE backend — a
5	single file on the compute node (configs/platform.yaml ->
6	observability.mlflow.tracking_uri, default "sqlite:///mlflow.db"). No remote
7	tracking server, no SaaS — nothing leaves the machine.
8	
9	(MLflow 3 put the legacy filesystem store, e.g. "mlruns/", into maintenance
10	mode; it refuses writes without MLFLOW_ALLOW_FILE_STORE=true and receives no
11	new features. SQLite is the supported local backend and is equally
12	data-sovereign — one file, no daemon.)
13	
14	The SQLite backend needs NO running daemon: experiment data is written
15	directly to the .db file. A server is only needed to *view* runs, via
16	`mlflow ui --backend-store-uri <uri>` (see launch_ui_command()).
17	
18	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): callers should tag every run with the
19	platform run_id so it cross-references PostgresSaver/Phoenix/PROV-DM:
20	    mlflow.set_tag("run_id", state["run_id"])
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import os
26	from pathlib import Path
27	
28	import mlflow
29	
30	from ..config import load_platform_config, repo_root, require_local_uri
31	
32	# Any non-sqlite backing-store URI passes through untouched. Includes DB schemes
33	# (postgres/postgresql/mysql/mssql) — a bare `postgres://` was previously not
34	# recognized and got mangled into a bogus local path (#253).
35	_REMOTE_SCHEMES = (
36	    "http://", "https://", "file:",
37	    "postgresql:", "postgres:", "mysql:", "mysql+", "mssql:", "mssql+",
38	)
39	_SQLITE_PREFIX = "sqlite:///"
40	
41	
42	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
43	    """
44	    Resolve the MLflow tracking URI from platform config.
45	
46	    A relative SQLite path ("sqlite:///mlflow.db") or a bare relative directory
47	    ("mlruns") is resolved to an absolute location under the repo root, so the
48	    store is independent of the working directory. Absolute paths and remote
49	    schemes (http:, postgresql:, file:) pass through untouched.
50	    """
51	    cfg = load_platform_config()["observability"]["mlflow"]
52	    uri = cfg.get("tracking_uri", f"{_SQLITE_PREFIX}mlflow.db")
53	    if uri.startswith(_SQLITE_PREFIX):
54	        path = uri[len(_SQLITE_PREFIX):]
55	        if not os.path.isabs(path):
56	            path = str(repo_root() / path)
57	        return f"{_SQLITE_PREFIX}{path}"
58	    if uri.startswith(_REMOTE_SCHEMES) or os.path.isabs(uri):
59	        return uri
60	    return str(repo_root() / uri)
61	
62	
63	def configure_mlflow() -> str:
64	    """
65	    Point MLflow at the local store and select the platform experiment.
66	
67	    Idempotent — safe to call at the start of every run. Returns the resolved
68	    tracking URI for logging/verification.
69	    """
70	    cfg = load_platform_config()["observability"]["mlflow"]
71	    uri = get_tracking_uri()
72	    # ADR-0002 defense-in-depth: refuse to point MLflow at a remote tracking server
73	    # even if config validation was bypassed — experiment metadata is SCF-derived
74	    # and must not leave the box (pass-5). config.py validates the raw value at load;
75	    # this guards the resolved value at the actual set_tracking_uri() call site.
76	    require_local_uri(uri, "observability.mlflow")
77	    # A bare local directory (legacy file store) must exist; sqlite/remote do not.
78	    if not uri.startswith(_SQLITE_PREFIX) and not uri.startswith(_REMOTE_SCHEMES):
79	        Path(uri).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
80	    mlflow.set_tracking_uri(uri)
81	    mlflow.set_experiment(cfg.get("experiment_name", "hc-grc"))
82	    return uri
83	
84	
85	def launch_ui_command() -> str:
86	    """Return the shell command to view runs in the MLflow UI."""
87	    return f"mlflow ui --backend-store-uri {get_tracking_uri()}"
88	
89	
90	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
91	    resolved = configure_mlflow()
92	    print(f"MLflow configured. tracking_uri={resolved}")
93	    print(f"View runs with: {launch_ui_command()}")
94

1	"""
2	Tests for the Phoenix/OpenTelemetry bootstrap (ADR-0009).
3	
4	Offline-safe: these never register a real global tracer provider (that would
5	mutate process-global OTel state for the rest of the suite). They exercise the
6	guards, the idempotency cache, and the run_id context manager.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10

1	"""
2	reasoning_client — the ADR-0016 LLM execution-routing seam.
3	
4	A single abstraction that isolates LLM auth/transport from agent logic. Agents
5	declare *what* to reason about and at *what stakes tier*; this seam decides
6	*where* the call runs, how it authenticates, how it retries, times out, and how
7	it is traced. No agent imports an LLM client directly — that coupling lives here
8	and only here (ADR-0016 §"Implementation seam").
9	
10	Tiers (routing is by stakes, per ADR-0016 / agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md):
11	
12	    Tier.T1  — Deterministic ML. No LLM. complete() on T1 is a routing bug and
13	               raises: T1 work is computation, never inference.
14	    Tier.T2  — Local LLM on the Mac mini, 24/7 (Ollama). Bulk / low-stakes
15	               reasoning: per-pair classification, triage, drafting, review.
16	    Tier.T3  — Frontier Claude Max 20x via claude-agent-sdk. The `claude` CLI's
17	               subscription OAuth (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) is the ONLY auth path
18	               — NO Anthropic API key. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is scrubbed from the SDK
19	               subprocess env so a stray key can never spill to the metered API
20	               (ADR-0016). Reserved for high-stakes judgments. Bursty / queued.
21	
22	Reliability (hardening pass 1):
23	  - Every call has a per-tier timeout (#145, #158) so a hung backend can never
24	    block a synchronous LangGraph node forever.
25	  - Transient failures are retried with bounded exponential backoff (#157).
26	  - Every call is wrapped in an OpenTelemetry span carrying run_id/agent_id/tier
27	    (OpenInference LLM conventions) so BOTH tiers are visible in Phoenix — T3 is
28	    not LangChain-instrumented, so without this it would be invisible (#150, #168).
29	    Spans are cheap no-ops when no tracer provider is registered (offline/tests).
30	  - complete_json uses Ollama's native JSON mode on T2 and a balanced-brace
31	    extractor, instead of a greedy regex that mangled multi-value replies (#159).
32	
33	Data sovereignty (ADR-0002 as amended by ADR-0016): persistent artifacts never
34	leave the machine; T3 permits transient, non-retained inference over the specific
35	SCF-derived text needed for a single call.
36	
37	Public surface:
38	    complete(tier, prompt, system=..., run_id=..., agent_id=...) -> str
39	    complete_json(tier, prompt, ...) -> dict | list
40	    is_available(tier) -> bool
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import asyncio
46	import json
47	import os
48	import threading
49	import time
50	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
51	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
52	from enum import Enum
53	from functools import lru_cache
54	from typing import Any
55	
56	from opentelemetry import trace
57	
58	# ── Tiers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59	
60	
61	class Tier(str, Enum):
62	    """Execution tier, by stakes. See agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md."""
63	
64	    T1 = "t1"  # deterministic ML — no LLM
65	    T2 = "t2"  # local Ollama
66	    T3 = "t3"  # frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK
67	
68	
69	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
70	    """Raised when a reasoning call cannot be completed (backend down, timeout, T1 misuse)."""
71	
72	
73	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
74	    """A non-transient failure (model missing, auth, CLI absent). Never retried."""
75	
76	
77	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
78	    """A Tier-3 subscription rate window. Retried with long backpressure, never
79	    spilled to a metered API (ADR-0016)."""
80	
81	
82	# ── Configuration (env-overridable; local-first defaults) ─────────────────────
83	
84	
85	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
86	    val = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
87	    return val or default
88	
89	
90	T2_MODEL = _env("HCGRC_T2_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
91	T2_BASE_URL = _env("HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
92	T3_MODEL = os.environ.get("HCGRC_T3_MODEL", "").strip() or None
93	
94	# Per-tier hard timeouts (seconds). T3 is bursty/queued so it gets more headroom.
95	T2_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT", "120"))
96	T3_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T3_TIMEOUT", "300"))
97	
98	# Tier-2 (Ollama) decoding controls. num_ctx avoids silent truncation at the 2048
99	# default (#196); seed makes greedy decoding reproducible (#197); num_predict caps
100	# output so a runaway/JSON-mode generation hits a bound before the hard timeout (#198).
101	T2_NUM_CTX = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_CTX", "8192"))
102	T2_SEED = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_SEED", "0"))
103	T2_NUM_PREDICT = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_PREDICT", "2048"))
104	
105	# Bounded retry with exponential backoff on transient failures. Clamped >= 0 so a
106	# negative override cannot produce an empty loop that raises None (#208).
107	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
108	BACKOFF_BASE = float(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5"))
109	# Tier-3 rate-window backpressure: long, capped waits — queue and resume, never
110	# fail fast onto a metered path.
111	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = float(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30"))
112	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
113	
114	# Truncation cap for prompt/response text recorded on spans (local Phoenix only).
115	_SPAN_TEXT_CAP = 8000
116	
117	_tracer = trace.get_tracer("hcgrc.reasoning_client")
118	
119	# Shared executor for enforcing sync-call timeouts. Not shut down: a hung backend
120	# call leaks one daemon worker rather than blocking the node (a ThreadPoolExecutor
121	# context-manager exit would join the hung thread, defeating the timeout).
122	_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS = 8
123	_timeout_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(
124	    max_workers=_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout"
125	)
126	
127	# In-flight worker accounting (#272). A timed-out call abandons (does NOT cancel)
128	# its worker — ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread blocked in
129	# llm.invoke/asyncio.run — so a truly-hung backend leaks that slot until (if ever)
130	# the call returns. Without a bound, accumulated leaks saturate the 8-slot pool and
131	# every subsequent submit silently queues behind work that never starts, converting
132	# ALL traffic into guaranteed timeouts. We track in-flight slots and fail FAST and
133	# LOUD with a distinct error when the pool is saturated, instead of degrading into a
134	# silent cliff. The per-tier inner timeouts (T2 HTTP timeout, T3 asyncio.wait_for)
135	# normally let workers self-terminate, so this guard only fires under genuine leaks.
136	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
137	_inflight = 0
138	
139	
140	def _submit_bounded(fn):
141	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if all worker slots are already in
142	    flight (i.e. leaked to hung backends). Decrements the count when the future
143	    completes — a leaked worker keeps its slot counted until it finally returns."""
144	    global _inflight
145	    with _inflight_lock:
146	        if _inflight >= _TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS:
147	            raise ReasoningError(
148	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS} "
149	                "workers in-flight) — hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
150	            )
151	        _inflight += 1
152	
153	    def _release(_):
154	        global _inflight
155	        with _inflight_lock:
156	            _inflight -= 1
157	
158	    # Submit only after incrementing, but if submit() itself raises (e.g. the pool
159	    # was shut down — "cannot schedule new futures after shutdown") the done-callback
160	    # never fires, so decrement here to avoid permanently leaking the slot and
161	    # eventually false-saturating the pool (pass-5).
162	    try:
163	        future = _timeout_pool.submit(fn)
164	    except BaseException:
165	        with _inflight_lock:
166	            _inflight -= 1
167	        raise
168	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
169	    return future
170	
171	
172	# ── Timeout helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	
174	
175	def _with_timeout(fn, timeout: float, what: str):
176	    """Run a sync callable with a hard timeout; raise ReasoningError on overrun.
177	
178	    Propagates the current OpenTelemetry context into the worker thread so spans
179	    created by fn (e.g. the LangChain ChatOllama span) nest under the active
180	    reasoning.complete span instead of being orphaned (#238).
181	    """
182	    from opentelemetry import context as otel_context
183	
184	    ctx = otel_context.get_current()
185	
186	    def _run():
187	        token = otel_context.attach(ctx)
188	        try:
189	            return fn()
190	        finally:
191	            otel_context.detach(token)
192	
193	    future = _submit_bounded(_run)
194	    try:
195	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
196	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
197	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
198	
199	
200	def _run_sync(coro: Any) -> Any:
201	    """Run a coroutine to completion from sync code, even inside a running loop."""
202	    try:
203	        asyncio.get_running_loop()
204	    except RuntimeError:
205	        return asyncio.run(coro)
206	    # Bound the cross-thread wait so a starved/leaked pool can't deadlock a node
207	    # forever (#227). The inner coroutine already has its own asyncio timeout; this
208	    # is a backstop slightly beyond it. _submit_bounded fails fast if the pool is
209	    # already saturated with leaked workers rather than queueing forever (#272).
210	    return _submit_bounded(lambda: asyncio.run(coro)).result(timeout=T3_TIMEOUT + 30)
211	
212	
213	# ── Tier 2: local Ollama ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
214	
215	
216	@lru_cache(maxsize=16)
217	def _ollama(model: str, temperature: float, json_mode: bool) -> Any:
218	    """Construct (and cache) a ChatOllama client for the local serving stack."""
219	    try:
220	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
221	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - dependency guard
222	        raise ReasoningError(
223	            "langchain-ollama is not installed — Tier 2 backend unavailable."
224	        ) from exc
225	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
226	        "model": model,
227	        "temperature": temperature,
228	        "base_url": T2_BASE_URL,
229	        "num_ctx": T2_NUM_CTX,         # avoid silent truncation at the 2048 default (#196)
230	        "seed": T2_SEED,               # reproducible greedy decoding (#197)
231	        "client_kwargs": {"timeout": T2_TIMEOUT},  # native HTTP timeout (#195)
232	    }
233	    if json_mode:
234	        # Native structured-output: Ollama constrains decoding to valid JSON,
235	        # far more reliable than instructing + regex-extracting (#159, #175). Do
236	        # NOT cap num_predict here — format=json terminates at the end of a valid
237	        # JSON value, and a num_predict cap would truncate it into INVALID JSON
238	        # (#231). The hard timeout remains the runaway backstop.
239	        kwargs["format"] = "json"
240	    else:
241	        kwargs["num_predict"] = T2_NUM_PREDICT  # bound free-text output length (#198)
242	    return ChatOllama(**kwargs)
243	
244	
245	def _t2_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float, model: str,
246	                 json_mode: bool) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
247	    llm = _ollama(model, temperature, json_mode)
248	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
249	    if system:
250	        messages.append(("system", system))
251	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
252	
253	    def _call():
254	        resp = llm.invoke(messages)
255	        content = getattr(resp, "content", resp)
256	        text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
257	        return text, getattr(resp, "usage_metadata", None)  # token counts (#279)
258	
259	    try:
260	        # Give the outer future timeout margin over the inner HTTP timeout
261	        # (client_kwargs timeout == T2_TIMEOUT) so the inner socket timeout wins and
262	        # the worker self-terminates cleanly, instead of racing exactly and risking
263	        # an abandoned (leaked) worker — mirrors the T3_TIMEOUT + 30 backstop (#272).
264	        text, usage = _with_timeout(
265	            _call, T2_TIMEOUT + 10, f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL})"
266	        )
267	        text = text.strip()
268	        if not text:
269	            # An empty/whitespace reply is not a valid result — surface it as a
270	            # retryable error rather than silently returning "" and corrupting a
271	            # JSON caller or recording an empty high-stakes answer (#233).
272	            raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) returned an empty reply")
273	        return text, {"model": model, "usage": usage}
274	    except ReasoningError:
275	        raise
276	    except Exception as exc:
277	        msg = str(exc).lower()
278	        # A missing model is permanent — retrying just wastes backoff (#194).
279	        if "not found" in msg or "no such model" in msg or "try pulling" in msg:
280	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
281	                f"Tier 2 model {model!r} not available on Ollama: {exc}"
282	            ) from exc
283	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) call failed: {exc}") from exc
284	
285	
286	# ── Tier 3: frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK ─────────────────────────────────
287	
288	
289	def _scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
290	    """
291	    Environment for the Agent SDK subprocess with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY removed.
292	
293	    ADR-0016: execution runs entirely on the fixed Claude Max subscription via the
294	    `claude` CLI's OAuth token. A metered Anthropic API key must NEVER be used — if
295	    one is present in the process env it would silently bill per-token, so we strip
296	    it before the SDK ever sees it.
297	    """
298	    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k != "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"}
299	
300	
301	async def _t3_async(prompt: str, system: str | None, max_turns: int,
302	                    model: str | None) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
303	    try:
304	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
305	            AssistantMessage,
306	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
307	            ResultMessage,
308	            TextBlock,
309	            query,
310	        )
311	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - dependency guard
312	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
313	            "claude-agent-sdk is not installed — Tier 3 backend unavailable."
314	        ) from exc
315	
316	    opts = ClaudeAgentOptions(
317	        max_turns=max_turns,
318	        system_prompt=system,
319	        allowed_tools=[],          # pure reasoning — no filesystem/command access
320	        env=_scrubbed_env(),       # ADR-0016: no metered API key (#146)
321	        setting_sources=[],        # don't load ANY on-disk settings into the
322	                                   # subprocess — keep the sandbox closed (#12)
323	        permission_mode="dontAsk",  # never wait on an interactive permission prompt
324	                                    # in this headless, single-turn, no-tools path —
325	                                    # deny if not pre-approved so any attempted tool
326	                                    # use fails fast instead of hanging until the
327	                                    # T3_TIMEOUT backstop (#274)
328	    )
329	    if model:
330	        opts.model = model
331	
332	    chunks: list[str] = []
333	    meta: dict[str, Any] = {"model": model}  # real model id if pinned, else unknown
334	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
335	
336	    async def _drain():
337	        nonlocal result_error
338	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=opts):
339	            # The SDK emits a first-class RateLimitEvent ahead of / instead of a
340	            # ResultMessage error. ONLY rate_limit_info.status == 'rejected' is a hard
341	            # rate window — 'allowed'/'allowed_warning' are informational and must NOT
342	            # trigger the 30s+ backpressure path (pass-6, SDK contract). Keep a generic
343	            # status=='rejected' fallback for other rejected-message shapes (#266).
344	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
345	                rl_status = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
346	                if rl_status == "rejected":
347	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "errors": ["RateLimitEvent"],
348	                                    "stop_reason": "rate_limit"}
349	                continue
350	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
351	                result_error = {"status": 429, "errors": ["rejected"], "stop_reason": "rate_limit"}
352	                continue
353	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
354	                if getattr(msg, "model", None):
355	                    meta["model"] = msg.model  # the actual model the run used (#204)
356	                for block in msg.content:
357	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
358	                        chunks.append(block.text)
359	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
360	                if getattr(msg, "usage", None):
361	                    meta["usage"] = msg.usage  # token usage for the span (#203)
362	                if getattr(msg, "total_cost_usd", None) is not None:
363	                    meta["cost_usd"] = msg.total_cost_usd
364	                # Capture the model's stop reason on SUCCESSFUL runs too (not just
365	                # errors) so the span records why generation ended — end_turn vs
366	                # max_tokens vs tool_use (OpenInference llm.finish_reason) (pass-5).
367	                if getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None):
368	                    meta["stop_reason"] = msg.stop_reason
369	                # The CLI reports API failures (incl. rate windows) by COMPLETING
370	                # the stream with is_error=True / api_error_status set, NOT by
371	                # raising. Capture it so a failed run is not returned as success
372	                # (pass-4 #1).
373	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False):
374	                    result_error = {
375	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
376	                        "errors": getattr(msg, "errors", None),
377	                        "stop_reason": getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
378	                    }
379	
380	    await asyncio.wait_for(_drain(), timeout=T3_TIMEOUT)
381	
382	    if result_error is not None:
383	        status = result_error.get("status")
384	        detail = result_error.get("errors") or result_error.get("stop_reason") or status
385	        if status in (429, 529):
386	            raise RateLimitError(f"Tier 3 rate window (api_error_status={status}): {detail}")
387	        if status in (401, 403):
388	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"Tier 3 auth failure ({status}): {detail}")
389	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 run reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
390	
391	    return "".join(chunks).strip(), meta
392	
393	
394	def _t3_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, max_turns: int,
395	                 model: str | None) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
396	    try:
397	        text, meta = _run_sync(_t3_async(prompt, system, max_turns, model))
398	        if not text:
399	            # An empty frontier reply is not a valid high-stakes result — retry
400	            # rather than return "" as success (#246).
401	            raise ReasoningError("Tier 3 (Agent SDK) returned an empty reply")
402	        return text, meta
403	    except ReasoningError:
404	        raise
405	    except (asyncio.TimeoutError, FuturesTimeout) as exc:
406	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) timed out after {T3_TIMEOUT:.0f}s") from exc
407	    except Exception as exc:
408	        # Classify on both the message and the exception type name — substring
409	        # matching alone is fragile across SDK versions (#229).
410	        sig = f"{type(exc).__name__} {exc}".lower()
411	        # Subscription rate window — queue and resume with long backpressure,
412	        # never spill to a metered API (ADR-0016, #185).
413	        if any(k in sig for k in ("rate limit", "rate_limit", "ratelimit", "429",
414	                                  "overloaded", "usage limit", "too many requests")):
415	            raise RateLimitError(f"Tier 3 rate window: {exc}") from exc
416	        # CLI absent / auth failure — permanent, not worth retrying (#194).
417	        if any(k in sig for k in ("not found", "notfound", "command not found",
418	                                  "no such file", "auth", "credential", "401",
419	                                  "403", "unauthor")):
420	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) unavailable: {exc}") from exc
421	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) call failed: {exc}") from exc
422	
423	
424	# ── Public API ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
425	
426	
427	def _attempt(tier: Tier, prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float,
428	             max_turns: int, model: str | None, json_mode: bool) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
429	    if tier is Tier.T2:
430	        return _t2_complete(prompt, system, temperature, model or T2_MODEL, json_mode)
431	    return _t3_complete(prompt, system, max_turns, model or T3_MODEL)
432	
433	
434	def _record_usage(span, usage: Any) -> None:
435	    """Set OpenInference token-count attributes from a usage dict/object (#203)."""
436	    if not usage:
437	        return
438	
439	    def g(key: str):
440	        return usage.get(key) if isinstance(usage, dict) else getattr(usage, key, None)
441	
442	    prompt_toks = g("input_tokens")
443	    completion_toks = g("output_tokens")
444	    # Anthropic/Agent-SDK report cache tokens in SEPARATE fields excluded from
445	    # input_tokens; on a cached-prefix run they can dominate real input. Fold them
446	    # into the prompt/total counts (and emit the OpenInference cache slots) so
447	    # llm.token_count.total reflects all tokens processed, not an undercount on the
448	    # very tier where usage visibility matters most (#280). Real spend is still
449	    # tracked separately via llm.cost_usd.
450	    cache_read = g("cache_read_input_tokens")
451	    cache_write = g("cache_creation_input_tokens")
452	    if cache_read is not None:
453	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt_details.cache_read", cache_read)
454	    if cache_write is not None:
455	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt_details.cache_write", cache_write)
456	
457	    prompt_total = None
458	    if prompt_toks is not None or cache_read is not None or cache_write is not None:
459	        prompt_total = (prompt_toks or 0) + (cache_read or 0) + (cache_write or 0)
460	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt", prompt_total)
461	    if completion_toks is not None:
462	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.completion", completion_toks)
463	    if prompt_total is not None and completion_toks is not None:
464	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.total", prompt_total + completion_toks)
465	
466	
467	def complete(
468	    tier: Tier,
469	    prompt: str,
470	    *,
471	    system: str | None = None,
472	    temperature: float = 0.0,
473	    max_turns: int = 1,
474	    model: str | None = None,
475	    run_id: str | None = None,
476	    agent_id: str | None = None,
477	    _json_mode: bool = False,
478	) -> str:
479	    """
480	    Run a single reasoning call at the given stakes tier and return the text.
481	
482	    Times out per tier, retries transient failures with backoff, and emits an
483	    OpenTelemetry span (run_id/agent_id/tier) so the call is visible in Phoenix.
484	
485	    Raises ReasoningError on T1 misuse or after retries are exhausted. Callers
486	    that must keep the graph moving (dry-runs) should catch and degrade.
487	    """
488	    if tier is Tier.T1:
489	        raise ReasoningError(
490	            "Tier.T1 is deterministic ML — it has no LLM. A reasoning_client call "
491	            "on T1 is a routing bug; the work belongs in computation, not inference."
492	        )
493	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
494	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning disabled via HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING")
495	
496	    # Fall back to the run_id stamped in OTel baggage by run_trace_context, so a
497	    # seam span carries the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it (#239).
498	    if run_id is None:
499	        try:
500	            from opentelemetry import baggage
501	            run_id = baggage.get_baggage("hcgrc.run_id") or None
502	        except Exception:
503	            run_id = None
504	
505	    # Known model id only — never fabricate one (#204). For a T3 run on the
506	    # subscription default, the real model is read back from the response.
507	    known_model = model or (T2_MODEL if tier is Tier.T2 else T3_MODEL)
508	    with _tracer.start_as_current_span("reasoning.complete") as span:
509	        # For T2, LangChainInstrumentor emits a child ChatOllama span under this
510	        # one — intentional parent/child nesting (the seam-level call + the
511	        # provider call), not redundant double-tracing (#214). T3 has no LangChain
512	        # span, so this is the only record of the frontier call.
513	        # OpenInference LLM conventions + HC-GRC cross-store correlation.
514	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", "LLM")
515	        span.set_attribute("llm.provider", "ollama" if tier is Tier.T2 else "anthropic")
516	        if known_model:
517	            span.set_attribute("llm.model_name", known_model)
518	        span.set_attribute("hcgrc.tier", tier.value)
519	        if run_id:
520	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.run_id", run_id)
521	            span.set_attribute("session.id", run_id)
522	        if agent_id:
523	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.agent_id", agent_id)
524	        if system:
525	            # Record the system prompt too — without it the trace input is
526	            # incomplete and the call is not reproducible from the span (#240).
527	            span.set_attribute("llm.system", system[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
528	        span.set_attribute("input.value", prompt[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
529	
530	        last_exc: ReasoningError | None = None
531	        # Independent retry budgets so a mix of transient and rate-limit errors
532	        # cannot exhaust each other's allowance prematurely (#228).
533	        transient_attempts = 0
534	        rate_attempts = 0
535	        while True:
536	            try:
537	                out, meta = _attempt(tier, prompt, system, temperature, max_turns, model, _json_mode)
538	                if meta.get("model"):
539	                    span.set_attribute("llm.model_name", meta["model"])
540	                _record_usage(span, meta.get("usage"))
541	                if meta.get("cost_usd") is not None:
542	                    span.set_attribute("llm.cost_usd", meta["cost_usd"])
543	                if meta.get("stop_reason"):
544	                    span.set_attribute("llm.finish_reason", meta["stop_reason"])  # (pass-5)
545	                span.set_attribute("output.value", out[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
546	                span.set_attribute("llm.retries", transient_attempts + rate_attempts)
547	                return out
548	            except PermanentReasoningError as exc:
549	                # Deterministic failure — do not retry (#194).
550	                last_exc = exc
551	                break
552	            except RateLimitError as exc:
553	                last_exc = exc
554	                if rate_attempts >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
555	                    break
556	                sleep_s = RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate_attempts + 1)
557	                span.add_event("rate_limit_backpressure",
558	                               {"attempt": rate_attempts, "sleep_s": sleep_s})  # actual wait (#273)
559	                time.sleep(sleep_s)
560	                rate_attempts += 1
561	            except ReasoningError as exc:
562	                last_exc = exc
563	                if transient_attempts >= MAX_RETRIES:
564	                    break
565	                span.add_event("retry", {"attempt": transient_attempts, "error": str(exc)[:300]})
566	                time.sleep(BACKOFF_BASE ** transient_attempts)
567	                transient_attempts += 1
568	        # Exhausted retries.
569	        span.record_exception(last_exc)
570	        span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(last_exc))
571	        raise last_exc
572	
573	
574	def complete_json(
575	    tier: Tier,
576	    prompt: str,
577	    *,
578	    system: str | None = None,
579	    temperature: float = 0.0,
580	    max_turns: int = 1,
581	    model: str | None = None,
582	    run_id: str | None = None,
583	    agent_id: str | None = None,
584	) -> Any:
585	    """
586	    Like complete(), but parse the model's reply as JSON.
587	
588	    On T2 this uses Ollama's native JSON mode (constrained decoding). The reply is
589	    then parsed, falling back to a balanced-brace extractor for models that wrap
590	    JSON in prose/fences. A parse failure is retried (re-generating the reply) up
591	    to MAX_RETRIES — the retry budget belongs here, not only in complete() (#230).
592	    Each retry bumps the decoding temperature (#275): with a fixed seed + temp 0,
593	    Ollama decoding is deterministic, so re-issuing the identical prompt would yield
594	    a byte-identical bad reply and the regenerate-on-parse-failure contract could
595	    never recover. A small bump yields a distinct cached client and a genuinely
596	    different generation. (T3 ignores temperature and is non-deterministic anyway.)
597	    Raises ReasoningError if no valid JSON can be recovered.
598	    """
599	    json_system = (system + "\n\n" if system else "") + (
600	        "Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON value and no other text, "
601	        "no markdown fences, no commentary."
602	    )
603	    last_exc: ReasoningError | None = None
604	    # Span the parse-retry loop so bad-JSON regenerations are visible in Phoenix
605	    # rather than silent (#269).
606	    with _tracer.start_as_current_span("reasoning.complete_json") as span:
607	        span.set_attribute("hcgrc.tier", tier.value)
608	        if run_id:
609	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.run_id", run_id)
610	            span.set_attribute("session.id", run_id)  # mirror complete() (pass-6)
611	        if agent_id:
612	            # Mirror complete()'s child span so the parent carries the same
613	            # correlation id, not just its children (pass-5).
614	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.agent_id", agent_id)
615	        for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES + 1):
616	            # Vary decoding across regenerations so a retry can actually differ from
617	            # the prior attempt (#275). A plain min(1.0, temp + bump) saturates at
618	            # 1.0 for high input temperatures, making retries identical again
619	            # (pass-5); when the upward bump would exceed 1.0, bump DOWNWARD instead
620	            # so every attempt gets a distinct, in-range temperature.
621	            if attempt == 0:
622	                attempt_temp = temperature
623	            else:
624	                bump = 0.2 * attempt
625	                attempt_temp = (temperature + bump if temperature + bump <= 1.0
626	                                else max(0.0, temperature - bump))
627	            try:
628	                raw = complete(
629	                    tier, prompt, system=json_system, temperature=attempt_temp,
630	                    max_turns=max_turns, model=model, run_id=run_id, agent_id=agent_id,
631	                    _json_mode=True,
632	                )
633	            except ReasoningError as exc:
634	                # A backend failure from complete() (rate limit / permanent / other,
635	                # already internally retried) is NOT a JSON parse failure — record it
636	                # on this span too (else only complete()'s child span has it) and
637	                # propagate (pass-5).
638	                span.record_exception(exc)
639	                span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(exc))
640	                raise
641	            try:
642	                result = _extract_json(raw)
643	                span.set_attribute("json.parse_attempts", attempt + 1)
644	                return result
645	            except ReasoningError as exc:
646	                last_exc = exc  # bad/garbled JSON — regenerate and try again
647	                span.add_event("json_parse_failure",
648	                               {"attempt": attempt, "error": str(exc)[:300]})
649	        span.record_exception(last_exc)
650	        span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(last_exc))
651	        raise last_exc
652	
653	
654	def _extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
655	    """Recover a JSON value from a model reply that may include fences/prose."""
656	    candidate = text.strip()
657	    # Strip a ```json ... ``` fence if present.
658	    if candidate.startswith("```"):
659	        inner = candidate[3:]
660	        if inner[:4].lower() == "json":
661	            inner = inner[4:]
662	        candidate = inner.rsplit("```", 1)[0].strip()
663	    try:
664	        return json.loads(candidate)
665	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
666	        pass
667	    span = _first_balanced_json(candidate)
668	    if span is not None:
669	        try:
670	            return json.loads(span)
671	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
672	            raise ReasoningError(f"Reply was not valid JSON: {text[:200]!r}") from exc
673	    raise ReasoningError(f"No JSON value found in reply: {text[:200]!r}")
674	
675	
676	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
677	    """
678	    Return the first balanced {...} or [...] span, respecting string literals.
679	
680	    A greedy regex (`[\\{\\[].*[\\}\\]]`) grabs from the first brace to the LAST
681	    brace, which mangles replies containing multiple JSON values or braces in
682	    trailing prose. This walks the structure honestly (#159).
683	    """
684	    start = None
685	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
686	        if ch in "{[":
687	            start = i
688	            break
689	    if start is None:
690	        return None
691	    open_ch = text[start]
692	    close_ch = "}" if open_ch == "{" else "]"
693	    depth = 0
694	    in_str = False
695	    escaped = False
696	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
697	        ch = text[i]
698	        if in_str:
699	            if escaped:
700	                escaped = False
701	            elif ch == "\\":
702	                escaped = True
703	            elif ch == '"':
704	                in_str = False
705	            continue
706	        if ch == '"':
707	            in_str = True
708	        elif ch == open_ch:
709	            depth += 1
710	        elif ch == close_ch:
711	            depth -= 1
712	            if depth == 0:
713	                return text[start:i + 1]
714	    return None
715	
716	
717	def is_available(tier: Tier) -> bool:
718	    """
719	    Cheap, side-effect-light liveness check for a tier's backend, used by nodes
720	    that must degrade gracefully rather than fail the run.
721	
722	    Does NOT spend an LLM turn (the old probe burned a real Tier-3 frontier turn
723	    on every call, #174, and could hang with no timeout, #173/#30). T1 is always
724	    available (local computation). Honors HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING.
725	    """
726	    if tier is Tier.T1:
727	        return True
728	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
729	        return False
730	    if tier is Tier.T2:
731	        # Hit Ollama's /api/tags — no generation, short timeout — and confirm the
732	        # configured model is actually pulled, not merely that the server is up (#210).
733	        import json as _json
734	        import urllib.request
735	        try:
736	            with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{T2_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=3) as r:
737	                if r.status != 200:
738	                    return False
739	                names = [m.get("name", "") for m in _json.loads(r.read()).get("models", [])]
740	        except Exception:
741	            return False
742	        # Exact match only: a prefix match (e.g. configured llama3.1:8b vs a
743	        # pulled llama3.1:70b) would pass here but complete() would then fail as
744	        # "model not found" (#232). Ollama tags an untagged pull as ":latest".
745	        return any(n == T2_MODEL or n == f"{T2_MODEL}:latest" for n in names)
746	    if tier is Tier.T3:
747	        # The `claude` CLI must be present and an OAuth token / stored creds
748	        # available — no frontier turn spent.
749	        import shutil
750	        if shutil.which("claude") is None:
751	            return False
752	        if os.environ.get("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "").strip():
753	            return True
754	        creds = os.path.expanduser("~/.claude/.credentials.json")
755	        return os.path.exists(creds)
756	    return False

2	HC-GRC main LangGraph graph — Phase 0 + Phase 1 exploratory subgraph.
3	
4	Graph topology (Phase 0 + Phase 1):
5	
6	  orchestrator

8	  data_split          ← SHA-256 seeded 70/15/15 split (synthetic in Phase 0)
9	      ↓

31	Per ADR-0015: run_id propagates through thread_config to PostgresSaver

63	# Resilience is layered, NOT via node RetryPolicy (pass-4 #15): the reasoning_client

123	        },

136	            "exploratory_entry": "exploratory_phase",    # rejected → re-run EDA

139	    )

141	    # ── Phase 2 stub edge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
142	    # confirmatory_entry → gate_3 → gate_4 → gate_5 will be wired in Phase 2.
143	    # For now, confirmatory_entry routes to END so the graph compiles.
144	    builder.add_edge("confirmatory_entry", END)
145	
146	    # ── Compile ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147	    compile_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}
148	    if checkpointer is not None:
149	        compile_kwargs["checkpointer"] = checkpointer
150	
151	    return builder.compile(**compile_kwargs)
152	
153	
154	# ── Convenience runners ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
155	
156	def run_phase0_synthetic(run_id: str | None = None, checkpointer=None) -> dict[str, Any]:
157	    """
158	    Execute one complete Phase 0 synthetic run.
159	
160	    The graph will pause at Gate 1 and wait for operator input.
161	    This function is used in the governance dry-run.
162	
163	    For tests that need to skip the interrupt, use the graph's
164	    .invoke() with a pre-configured Command resume.
165	    """
166	    # interrupt() is a no-op without a checkpointer — the gate could not pause for
167	    # the operator. Default to the configured backend so the runner actually parks
168	    # at the gate (#200/#226).
169	    checkpointer = checkpointer or configured_checkpointer()
170	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
171	    state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
172	    bootstrap_observability()
173	    # Explicit recursion_limit bounds the Gate-2-rejected → exploratory loop so a
174	    # run that never converges terminates with GraphRecursionError instead of
175	    # looping unbounded (#179).
176	    thread_config = {
177	        "configurable": {"thread_id": state["run_id"]},
178	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
179	    }
180	    with run_trace_context(state["run_id"]):
181	        result = graph.invoke(state, config=thread_config)
182	    return result
183	
184	
185	def resume_run(run_id: str, decision: str, rationale: str, checkpointer,
186	               *, gate_id: str | None = None, checkpoint_id: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
187	    """
188	    Resume a run parked at a gate interrupt with the operator's decision.
189	
190	    The runners invoke the graph once and return when a gate's interrupt() parks
191	    it; this is the other half of the loop (#162). The operator's decision is
192	    delivered via the ADR-0014 governance channel and injected here as the
193	    interrupt's resume value, so the gate node observes
194	    {'gate_id': ..., 'decision': ..., 'rationale': ...} and continues.
195	
196	    Pass gate_id (the gate the operator was deciding on) so the gate-correlation
197	    guard in _run_gate can reject a stale/misrouted decision targeted at a gate
198	    other than the one currently parked (pass-4 #3). Without it the guard is
199	    unreachable. The ADR-0014 channel should record and pass back the gate_id it
200	    surfaced.
201	
202	    Requires the SAME checkpointer the run was started with (thread_id == run_id).
203	    """
204	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
205	    bootstrap_observability()
206	    config: dict[str, Any] = {
207	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
208	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
209	    }
210	    if checkpoint_id:
211	        config["configurable"]["checkpoint_id"] = checkpoint_id
212	    resume_value: dict[str, Any] = {"decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
213	    if gate_id is not None:
214	        resume_value["gate_id"] = gate_id
215	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
216	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config)
217	
218	
219	def run_phase1_dry_run(run_id: str | None = None, checkpointer=None) -> dict[str, Any]:
220	    """
221	    Execute a Phase 1 dry run through Gate 2.
222	
223	    Gate 1 and Gate 2 will pause for operator input. Data pipeline and P1-P5
224	    nodes will record stub_pending statuses (NotImplementedError caught). This
225	    verifies the Phase 1 graph topology before real SCF data is loaded.
226	    """
227	    checkpointer = checkpointer or configured_checkpointer()
228	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
229	    state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
230	    bootstrap_observability()
231	    # Explicit recursion_limit bounds the Gate-2-rejected → exploratory loop so a
232	    # run that never converges terminates with GraphRecursionError instead of
233	    # looping unbounded (#179).
234	    thread_config = {
235	        "configurable": {"thread_id": state["run_id"]},
236	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
237	    }
238	    with run_trace_context(state["run_id"]):
239	        result = graph.invoke(state, config=thread_config)
240	    return result

1	"""
2	HCGRCState — canonical LangGraph state for the HC-GRC research platform.
3	
4	All nodes read from and write to this TypedDict. Fields are append-only
5	where possible to preserve audit history within a run.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import uuid
11	from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal
12	
13	from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages
14	from typing_extensions import TypedDict
15	
16	# ── Phase labels ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
17	
18	Phase = Literal["phase_0", "phase_1", "phase_2"]
19	
20	# Gate identifiers 1–5
21	GateID = Literal["gate_1", "gate_2", "gate_3", "gate_4", "gate_5"]
22	
23	# Only these three decisions ever exist: the operator-response validator
24	# (_VALID_DECISIONS) rejects anything else, so the type reflects what can actually
25	# be written. ('pending' was never produced or routed on — removed pass-6.)
26	GateDecision = Literal["approved", "rejected", "deferred"]
27	
28	
29	# ── Reducers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	
31	
32	def _merge_artifacts(a: list[str], b: list[str]) -> list[str]:
33	    """Append b onto a, dropping any value already present in a OR earlier in b.
34	    Order-preserving, O(n+m). The previous `x not in set(a)` deduped against a but
35	    not within b, so a single agent returning a duplicate path slipped through —
36	    violating the 'deduped on merge' contract (pass-6)."""
37	    seen = set(a)
38	    out = list(a)
39	    for x in b:
40	        if x not in seen:
41	            seen.add(x)
42	            out.append(x)
43	    return out
44	
45	
46	def _merge_hypotheses(a: list[dict[str, Any]], b: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
47	    """Append b onto a, deduping by hypothesis 'id' (latest wins, position kept).
48	
49	    Entries without an 'id' are kept as-is. Prevents a Gate-2 reject → re-formalize
50	    loop from accumulating duplicate hypotheses into the pre-registered set."""
51	    merged = list(a)
52	    index = {h["id"]: i for i, h in enumerate(merged) if isinstance(h, dict) and h.get("id")}
53	    for h in b:
54	        hid = h.get("id") if isinstance(h, dict) else None
55	        if hid is not None and hid in index:
56	            merged[index[hid]] = h
57	        else:
58	            if hid is not None:
59	                index[hid] = len(merged)
60	            merged.append(h)
61	    return merged
62	
63	
64	# ── Core state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	
66	
67	class GateStatus(TypedDict):
68	    """Written exclusively by GateCoordinatorNode to avoid concurrent writes."""
69	
70	    gate_id: GateID
71	    decision: GateDecision
72	    rationale: str
73	    reviewer: str  # "operator" | "claude" | "synthetic"
74	    timestamp_utc: str  # ISO-8601
75	    run_id: str
76	
77	
78	class HCGRCState(TypedDict):
79	    """
80	    Canonical platform state. run_id propagates to all four stores:
81	      - MLflow  (tags.run_id)
82	      - Phoenix (trace attribute run_id)
83	      - PROV-DM (activity IRI contains run_id)
84	      - PostgresSaver (thread_id column)
85	
86	    See RUNBOOK.md for cross-store query procedures.
87	    """
88	
89	    # ── Identity ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90	    run_id: str  # UUID4, set at graph entry, propagated everywhere
91	    thread_id: str  # LangGraph thread ID (== run_id for single-run threads)
92	    phase: Phase
93	
94	    # ── Gate tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
95	    # Merge reducer: {**a, **b} ensures concurrent gate writes are safe by
96	    # construction. gate_coordinator_node is the canonical writer, but the
97	    # reducer protects against any future path that returns a partial update.
98	    # Fixes: ADR-0015 §75 serial guarantee (F1) + silent overwrite risk (F4).
99	    gate_status: Annotated[dict[GateID, GateStatus], lambda a, b: {**a, **b}]
100	
101	    # ── Failure events (written by gate nodes on rejection) ───────────────────
102	    # Agent Evolution monitors this store for routing re-optimization signals.
103	    failure_events: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
104	
105	    # ── Research artifacts (append-only) ──────────────────────────────────────
106	    hypotheses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
107	    findings: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
108	
109	    # ── Data split record ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
110	    # Un-reduced (last-write-wins) scalars. Written by exactly one node per phase —
111	    # data_split_node in Phase 0, DataStewardAgent in Phase 1 — never concurrently,
112	    # so last-write-wins is safe by sequencing, not by a reducer (#212). Immutable
113	    # after Gate 1. If these ever become concurrently written, add a reducer.
114	    data_split_manifest_hash: str | None  # SHA-256 of the raw manifest file
115	    data_split_seed: int | None  # derived from manifest hash (int.from_bytes)
116	    data_split_verified: bool  # True after idempotency assertion passes
117	
118	    # ── Phase 1 execution tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────
119	    # phase_1_ready: True after data pipeline writes validated splits to disk.
120	    # exploratory_complete: True after all P1-P5 nodes complete (or graceful stub).
121	    phase_1_ready: bool
122	    exploratory_complete: bool
123	
124	    # ── Data pipeline provenance ──────────────────────────────────────────────
125	    # Written by EmbeddingAgent after embedding run completes. Required at Gate 2
126	    # to confirm which model produced the embeddings used in exploratory analysis.
127	    # Keys: model_name, model_hash, corpus_dvc_hash, timestamp_utc
128	    embedding_manifest: dict[str, Any] | None
129	
130	    # ── Exploratory analysis artifacts (append, deduped) ──────────────────────
131	    # eda_artifacts: file paths to all EXP_* outputs from P1-P5 nodes.
132	    # Each node appends its artifact paths. Gate 3 verifies all 5 agents present.
133	    # Deduped on merge so a Gate-2 reject → re-run-exploratory loop cannot
134	    # accumulate the same artifact path repeatedly (#235). Membership tested against
135	    # a set so the merge is O(n+m), not O(n*m), as the artifact list grows (pass-5).
136	    # eda_agent_statuses: per-agent completion records.
137	    # Keys: agent_id, status ("completed"|"stub_pending"|"failed"), note, timestamp_utc
138	    eda_artifacts: Annotated[list[str], _merge_artifacts]
139	    eda_agent_statuses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
140	
141	    # ── Formalized hypothesis set (append, deduped by id) ─────────────────────
142	    # Written by HypothesisFormalizerAgent before Gate 2.
143	    # Each entry is a FormalHypothesis dict (see src/agents/hypothesis_formalizer).
144	    # Deduped by hypothesis id (latest wins) so a Gate-2 reject → re-formalize loop
145	    # cannot accumulate duplicate hypotheses into the set Gate 2 locks (pass-4 #2).
146	    hypothesis_set: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: _merge_hypotheses(a, b)]
147	
148	    # ── Orchestrator run-start grounding ──────────────────────────────────────
149	    # Produced by t00_orchestrator_node via the reasoning_client (Tier 2). The
150	    # orchestrator reasons about the run *inside the graph* rather than the work
151	    # being done by hand outside the framework (ADR-0016 — the agency contract).
152	    # None when the reasoning backend was unavailable (graceful degradation).
153	    run_grounding: str | None
154	
155	    # ── Escalation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
156	    escalation_issue_number: int | None  # GitHub issue number if parked
157	    escalation_reason: str | None
158	
159	    # ── LLM messages (for agent traces) ──────────────────────────────────────
160	    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]
161	
162	    # ── Provenance ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	    prov_activities: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
164	
165	
166	def initial_state(run_id: str | None = None) -> HCGRCState:
167	    """Return a clean initial state for a new run."""
168	    rid = run_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
169	    return HCGRCState(
170	        run_id=rid,
171	        thread_id=rid,
172	        phase="phase_0",
173	        gate_status={},
174	        failure_events=[],
175	        hypotheses=[],
176	        findings=[],
177	        data_split_manifest_hash=None,
178	        data_split_seed=None,
179	        data_split_verified=False,
180	        phase_1_ready=False,
181	        exploratory_complete=False,
182	        embedding_manifest=None,
183	        eda_artifacts=[],
184	        eda_agent_statuses=[],
185	        hypothesis_set=[],
186	        run_grounding=None,
187	        escalation_issue_number=None,
188	        escalation_reason=None,
189	        messages=[],
190	        prov_activities=[],
191	    )

1	"""
2	HC-GRC agent base types and contracts.
3	
4	All Phase 1 research agents inherit from BaseResearchAgent or implement the
5	same __call__ signature. The contract is:
6	
7	    agent(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]
8	
9	The return dict is a LangGraph partial state update — merged into HCGRCState
10	by the graph runtime. Agents must never mutate state in place.
11	
12	PROTECTED agents (P1–P5, statistical-analyst, hypothesis-formalizer) have
13	PROTECTED = True. Agent Evolution may not modify their prompts autonomously.
14	Any modification requires Escalation approval (ADR-0015 §77).
15	
16	Phase guard:
17	  - Exploratory phase: access train_ids + val_ids only
18	  - Confirmatory phase: access test_ids — only after Gate 2 approval
19	  - Agents check state["gate_status"]["gate_2"].decision == "approved" before
20	    touching test split. Violation is a SAP violation.
21	
22	EXP_ prefix convention:
23	  - All exploratory artifacts use EXP_ filename prefix
24	  - No p-value decision language in EXP_ outputs
25	  - Confirmatory scripts carry a SAP header with hypothesis ID and decision rule
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
31	from typing import Any, Literal
32	
33	from ..state import HCGRCState
34	
35	# ── Shared input types ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36	
37	class STRMMapping:
38	    """
39	    A single STRM mapping between two SCF controls.
40	
41	    In Phase 0 (synthetic): populated with placeholder values.
42	    In Phase 1+: populated from data/splits/*.parquet.
43	    """
44	    __slots__ = (
45	        "source_control_id", "target_control_id",
46	        "relationship_type", "strength_score",
47	        "source_domain", "target_domain",
48	        "source_framework", "target_framework",
49	    )

51	    def __init__(
52	        self,
53	        source_control_id: str,
54	        target_control_id: str,
55	        relationship_type: Literal["subset", "superset", "intersection", "equal", "no_relation"],
56	        strength_score: float | None = None,
57	        source_domain: str | None = None,
58	        target_domain: str | None = None,
59	        source_framework: str | None = None,
60	        target_framework: str | None = None,
61	    ) -> None:
62	        self.source_control_id = source_control_id
63	        self.target_control_id = target_control_id
64	        self.relationship_type = relationship_type
65	        self.strength_score = strength_score
66	        self.source_domain = source_domain
67	        self.target_domain = target_domain
68	        self.source_framework = source_framework
69	        self.target_framework = target_framework

73	    """Metadata for a single SCF control node."""

77	    )

79	    def __init__(

94	# ── Phase guard ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
95	
96	class SAPViolationError(Exception):
97	    """Raised when an agent attempts to access test data before Gate 2."""
98	    pass
99	
100	
101	def assert_gate2_approved(state: HCGRCState) -> None:
102	    """
103	    Raise SAPViolationError if Gate 2 has not been approved.
104	
105	    Must be called before any agent accesses test_ids or test split data.
106	    This is the primary enforcement mechanism for the pre-registration firewall.
107	    """
108	    gate_status = state.get("gate_status", {})
109	    gate_2 = gate_status.get("gate_2")
110	    if gate_2 is None or gate_2.get("decision") != "approved":
111	        raise SAPViolationError(
112	            "Attempted to access test split before Gate 2 approval. "
113	            "This is a SAP violation. Gate 2 status: "
114	            f"{gate_2.get('decision') if gate_2 else 'not_run'}. "
115	            "Do not access test_ids or confirmatory split until gate_2.decision == 'approved'."
116	        )
117	
118	
119	# ── Base agent ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
120	
121	class BaseResearchAgent(ABC):
122	    """
123	    Abstract base for all HC-GRC Phase 1 research agents.
124	
125	    Subclasses implement run_exploratory() and run_confirmatory() separately.
126	    The __call__ method dispatches based on state["phase"].
127	
128	    Protected agents set PROTECTED = True — checked by Agent Evolution before
129	    any prompt modification.
130	    """
131	
132	    PROTECTED: bool = False  # override in P1–P5, statistical-analyst, hypothesis-formalizer
133	    AGENT_ID: str = ""       # kebab-case, matches AGENT.md name field
134	    # Set True once the agent's run_* methods are real. While False, a
135	    # NotImplementedError is an expected Phase-0/1 stub; once True, a
136	    # NotImplementedError is a genuine bug the orchestrator must not silently
137	    # swallow as 'stub_pending' (#202).
138	    IMPLEMENTED: bool = False
139	
140	    def __call__(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
141	        """
142	        LangGraph-compatible call: returns a partial state update dict.
143	
144	        Dispatches to run_exploratory() or run_confirmatory() based on phase.
145	
146	        The canonical phase values written by the graph are phase_0 / phase_1 /
147	        phase_2 (see state.Phase). Exploratory analysis runs in phase_0/phase_1;
148	        confirmatory analysis runs in phase_2, where the Gate 2 SAP firewall
149	        (assert_gate2_approved) must hold before any test-split access. The legacy
150	        string aliases "exploratory"/"confirmatory" are accepted defensively.
151	        (Hardening pass 1, #17: phase_2 previously hit the else-branch and raised,
152	        so run_confirmatory and the SAP firewall were unreachable.)
153	        """
154	        phase = state.get("phase", "phase_0")
155	        if phase in ("phase_0", "phase_1", "exploratory"):
156	            return self.run_exploratory(state)
157	        elif phase in ("phase_2", "confirmatory"):
158	            assert_gate2_approved(state)
159	            return self.run_confirmatory(state)
160	        else:
161	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown phase: {phase!r}")
162	
163	    @abstractmethod
164	    def run_exploratory(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
165	        """
166	        Execute exploratory analysis on train + val splits.
167	
168	        All output artifact filenames must be prefixed EXP_.
169	        No p-value decision language in any exploratory output.
170	        Must not touch test_ids.
171	        """
172	        ...
173	
174	    @abstractmethod
175	    def run_confirmatory(self, state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
176	        """
177	        Execute confirmatory analysis on test split for one hypothesis.
178	
179	        Gate 2 approval is verified by __call__ before this is invoked.
180	        Every confirmatory script must carry a SAP header:
181	          # SAP header: hypothesis_id=H[x].[n], test=[named test],
182	          #             effect_size_threshold=[value], decision_rule=[criterion]
183	        """
184	        ...
185	
186	
187	# ── Base pipeline agent ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
188	
189	class BasePipelineAgent(ABC):
190	    """
191	    Abstract base for data pipeline agents (acquisition, curation, steward, embedding).
192	
193	    Pipeline agents prepare and version the research corpus. They do not perform
194	    exploratory or confirmatory analysis — they have no run_exploratory() /
195	    run_confirmatory() distinction. They run once at project initialization and
196	    re-run when the SCF corpus is updated.
197	
198	    Pipeline agents are never PROTECTED — they contain no analysis logic that
199	    could affect research conclusions. Agent Evolution may modify them without
200	    Escalation approval.
201	
202	    The run() return dict is a partial HCGRCState update, merged by the graph runtime.
203	    Agents must never mutate state in place.
204	    """
205	
206	    AGENT_ID: str = ""     # kebab-case, matches AGENT.md name field
207	    PROTECTED: bool = False  # never True for pipeline agents
208	    IMPLEMENTED: bool = False  # see BaseResearchAgent.IMPLEMENTED (#202)
209
